Tomographer

Tomographer is an end-to-end clustering redshift package for estimating redshift distributions and probability density functions from angular cross-correlations.

Installation

  1. Download the repository from Google Drive:

    <Google Drive Link>

  2. Install the package

    cd tomographer_beta
pip install -e .

Project Structure

tomographer_beta/
├── runtime_script/
│   ├── conf.ini                # Configuration file
│   ├── tomo.py                 # Main code
│   └── tomo.ipynb              # Interactive notebook
├── precalculated_data/         # Downloaded precalculated files
├── pyproject.toml              # Build configuration and package metadata (pip install entry point)
└── README.md

The precalculated_data directory includes the default configuration with 40 tomographic bins. We also provide an extended version with 160 bins on Zenodo:

https://zenodo.org/records/20155554

Using the 160-bin configuration requires additional data downloads. These are automatically handled by the code. Note that not all files on Zenodo will be downloaded, only the required files based on the selected configuration.

To download all precalculated files from Zenodo in one step and automatically organize them into the correct directory structure:

from importlib import resources
from tomographer import utils

filepath_handler = utils.FilePathHandler()
data_path = resources.files("precalculated_data")

filepath_handler.download_all()

Quick Start

Users will mainly interact with the configuration file conf.ini.

  1. Modify the configuration file:

    conf.ini

  2. Run the pipeline:

    tomo

    If the configuration file is located in another directory, run:

    tomo --conf /path/to/conf.ini

For users who would like to explore the code in more detail or visualize intermediate results, we also provide the notebook:

tomo.ipynb

Output

Tomographer produces:

  • Measurement file: OUTPUT_FILENAME.fits

  • Saved configuration file: OUTPUT_FILENAME.ini

  • Result plot: OUTPUT_FILENAME.pdf
